title: Cross-Merchant Card Enumeration Pattern with Clean Per-Transaction Profile
narrative: Compromised card credentials are systematically tested across multiple unrelated merchant categories in rapid succession while keeping individual transaction amounts and timing within normal bounds. The fraudster uses the stolen card details at 8-15 distinct merchants within a 24-hour window, appearing as legitimate shopping at each merchant individually. Card-level aggregation counters reveal the card is being used at 3-5x the cardholder's historical merchant diversity rate. Each transaction passes basic checks—amounts are reasonable, billing addresses match, email verified—but the card's footprint across the merchant ecosystem shows an explosion of distinct MCC codes and merchant IDs that no legitimate cardholder could generate organically in this timeframe. Device fingerprints show 4-7 unique devices associated with these transactions, indicating credential sharing across a fraud network rather than device theft.
indicators: C8 > 29 (p90 threshold), indicating card used across excessive distinct merchant-category combinations in rolling window
C11 > 27 (p90 threshold), showing card activity across far more unique merchants than baseline
C14 between 5 and 10, elevated count of distinct merchant-country pairings for single card
D1 = 0 OR D2 = 0 on 60%+ of transactions, indicating first-time device-card associations
card1_distinct_addr1 between 1 and 2, showing address consistency masks the cross-merchant abuse
TransactionAmt between 60 and 200 (p25-p75 range), amounts stay within normal spending corridor
C1 < 20, overall card transaction count remains below suspicion threshold per individual check
indicator_explanations: Card appears across an unusually high number of different merchant categories in recent history
Card is being accepted at far more unique merchants than typical cardholder shopping patterns
Card shows activity in multiple merchant-country combinations inconsistent with normal travel
Majority of transactions pair this card with devices never seen together before
Billing address remains consistent across transactions, creating appearance of legitimate activity
Transaction amounts fall within typical shopping ranges, avoiding high-dollar red flags
Total number of card uses stays moderate enough to bypass simple velocity rules
summary: Stolen card credentials are methodically tested across 8-15 unrelated merchants within 24 hours while keeping amounts, addresses, and per-transaction metrics unremarkable. The fraud surface emerges only when examining card-level merchant diversity counters showing 3-5x normal cross-merchant activity.
reasoning: No legitimate cardholder generates this merchant diversity in such a compressed timeframe while maintaining perfect address consistency and moderate spending. The pattern indicates organized credential testing across a fraud network's merchant access points, with each operator keeping their individual transactions clean to avoid single-merchant fraud detection.
